utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , vincent . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good . how about you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . so le- are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . vincent is a 56-year-old male here with abnormal lab findings . so , i've heard you were in the er , vincent , and they found that you had a low hemoglobin .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: were you having some dizziness and some lightheadedness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i was very lightheaded . i- i do n't know . very lightheaded .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you noticed bleeding from anywhere ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have not . i have n't hurt myself in quite a while . maybe a slight nick from a knife while chopping some onions , but nothing more than that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but no blood in your stools or-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any type of weight loss or decreased appetite or night sweats ? coughs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , s- slightly decreased appetite , but i wish i had some weight loss .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , okay . and how about any abdominal pain ? fever , chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , none of that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , any nausea or vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not really . yeah . maybe a bit of nausea .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i- sitting at the back of a car , that makes me nauseous at times .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , well , how are you doing in terms of your knee replacement . i know you had that done last year . that's going okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm , it seems okay . yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . you're walking around without a problem ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup , yup . just not good enough to run yet , but everything else works just fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , and i know a few years ago , you had , had that scare with the possible lung cancer , but then they did the biopsy and , and you've been fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup , yup . all good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: turned out to be benign .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . all right . well , let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam . so looking at you , you do n't appear in any distress . your heart is regular . your lungs sound nice and clear . you have some tenderness to the right lower quadrant to palpation of your abdomen . your lower extremities have no edema .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , all right . well , let's go ahead and look at your labs , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the hemoglobin . yeah , so your hemoglobin is 8.2 , which is quite low for somebody of your height and weight , so we'll have to look into that a , a little bit further . i know that they did the endoscopy in the emergency room . hey , dragon , show me the endoscope results .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . so it looks like you had some gastritis , which is just inflammation of your stomach and they also found a slight polyp , which i know that they biopsied and the results are pending at this time . um , so , you may have had some bleeding from the gastritis . it's not usual for people to have bleeding from that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , okay , well , hey , dragon , show me the anemia panel . okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: anyway , okay . well , vincent , i think , you know , in terms of , my impression of you is that you've had this newfound anemia and for that , i think that we should go ahead and put you on protonix , 40 milligrams , once a day to help with the gastritis . does that sound okay to you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it does . you're the doctor . i do n't know what it is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so that's just , uh , what we call a proton pump inhibitor which , uh , helps decrease the amount of acid secreted within your stomach .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: got it . makes sense .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , hey , dragon , order protonix , 40 milligrams , once a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'd like you to try to cut down on your caffeine 'cause that can also irritate your stomach . try not to take any ibuprofen and try to cut down on any alcohol intake , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup , yup . the coffee's the hard part .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . it always is . how about one , one , one eight-ounce cup a day ? okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and we'll go ahead and we'll see you in a couple weeks , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure thing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm going through , uh , i'll also order another , uh , cbc on you . hey , dragon , order a complete blood count .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . the nurse will be in soon . it's , you know , settle all that . i'll see you soon .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 56-year-old male presenting for evaluation of abnormal labs and low hemoglobin found in the emergency room.
The doctor asks if the patient has been experiencing dizziness or lightheadedness.
The patient confirms he has been very lightheaded.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed any bleeding.
The patient denies any bleeding other than a minor knife nick.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed blood in his stools.
The patient denies blood in his stools.
The doctor asks about weight loss, appetite changes, night sweats, and cough.
The patient reports a slightly decreased appetite and denies weight loss.
The doctor asks about abdominal pain, fever, and chills.
The patient denies abdominal pain, fever, and chills.
The doctor asks about nausea and vomiting.
The patient reports mild nausea.
The patient notes nausea when sitting in the back of a car.
Knee arthroplasty status.
The patient reports his knee is doing okay.
The doctor asks if the patient is walking without problems.
The patient confirms he is walking well but cannot run yet.
The patient had a benign lung biopsy a few years ago.
Physical exam findings include no distress, regular heart rhythm, clear lungs, right lower quadrant abdominal tenderness, and no lower extremity edema.
The patient's hemoglobin is 8.2.
Endoscopy showed gastritis and a polyp with pending biopsy results.
Anemia.
Protonix 40mg daily.
The doctor advises reducing caffeine, avoiding NSAIDs, and reducing alcohol intake.
The doctor recommends limiting caffeine to one eight-ounce cup per day.
The patient is scheduled for a follow-up visit in two weeks.
The doctor orders a repeat complete blood count.

case_description: abnormal labs for anemia; hx lobectomy and knee arthroplasty
